summary.headline: ADM reports Q2 2024 results with adjusted EPS of $1.03, affirming full-year guidance.
summary.narrative_summary: ADM announced its second quarter 2024 financial results, reporting net earnings of $486 million and adjusted net earnings of $508 million. The company's adjusted earnings per share was $1.03, down from the prior year period due to lower pricing and execution margins. Despite challenging market conditions, ADM affirmed its full-year guidance. Segment performance showed declines in Ag Services Oilseeds and Nutrition, while Carbohydrate Solutions showed improvement.

concerns: Slower farmer selling due to smaller crop in Mato Grosso
Higher logistics costs related to industry take-or-pay contracts
Increased supply from Brazil and Argentina shifting export competitiveness
Global soybean crush margins decreased due to more balanced supply and demand conditions
Lower soybean oil values caused by increased imports of used cooking oil

drivers: Strong demand for exports of ethanol supporting higher margins

facilities_mentioned.name: Decatur East
facilities_mentioned.context: Unplanned downtime negatively impacted Nutrition segment results

leadership_changes.name: Juan Luciano
leadership_changes.role: Chair of the Board and CEO

ma_or_capital_allocation.description: Returned significant cash to shareholders through repurchase efforts through the first two quarters.

extraction_notes: Numbers extracted from Exhibit 99.1; cover sheet only had Item references.
